_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate information (e.g. trust relationships and security policies) from a domain to identify vulnerabilities/misconfiguration
reference_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate the regulatory implications of proposed data processing activities, AI system deployments, or business model changes, providing compliance risk assessment before implementation.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-A-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-S-009 (DQ, cosine: 0.521), SP-S-003 (SP, cosine: 0.515).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.1/10 (raw 0.607). Shared vocabulary: [ability, evaluate]. DCWF-only terms [domain, identify, information, misconfiguration, policies, relationships] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, ai, assessment, business, changes, compliance] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.867 (P=0.866, R=0.867). Balanced P/R — the 16-word DCWF element and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Ability to evaluate information (e.g. trust relationships and security policies)" and "Ability to evaluate the regulatory implications of proposed data processing acti" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.535 < STS 0.607 (gap -0.072). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [domain, identify, information] and [activities, ai, assessment] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.9%. Shared tokens: [ability, evaluate]. DCWF-only: [domain, identify, information, misconfiguration, policies, relationships]. WIDAI-only: [activities, ai, assessment, business, changes, compliance].
